title: The Platform Economy for Young Minds

how-it-works.title: How This Works
content: Welcome to The Platform Economy for Young Minds.
In this book, you will read about apps, streaming services, creators, online platforms, and artificial intelligence.
Read the chapter text aloud in the upper reading section.
Tap the purple words to open glossary meanings and hear the word and its definition.
Use the recorder to record yourself reading, then play it back, clear it, or download it.
Complete the worksheet in the lower section after each chapter.
Move through the book one chapter at a time and build your understanding as you go.

platform.definition: A digital system that connects people or groups so they can exchange value, services, content, or products.
platform.simpleMeaning: An online place where people connect and do things.
platform.example: A learning app can be a platform.

creator.definition: A person who makes something of value such as a video, lesson, song, book, or digital product.
creator.simpleMeaning: Someone who makes useful or creative things.
creator.example: A teacher who makes online lessons is a creator.

artificial intelligence.definition: Technology that helps computers perform tasks such as generating ideas, analysing information, or assisting users.
artificial intelligence.simpleMeaning: Smart computer tools that help people do tasks.
artificial intelligence.example: Artificial intelligence can help organise information.

worksheet.title: Getting Ready
worksheet.prompt: What do you think this book will teach you about digital platforms, creators, and technology?

ch1.title: Chapter 1: The Digital World Around Us
content: The digital world is part of everyday life.
Many learners wake up to a phone alarm, watch videos online, listen to music through an app, send messages, search for information, and use digital tools for schoolwork.
All of these activities take place on [[platform]].
A [[platform]] is more than just a website or an app. It is a system that connects different groups of people so that they can exchange something of value.
Instead of owning every song or movie, people often pay for access.
This book explains how the platform economy works in a simple and engaging way.

platform.definition: A system that connects people and allows them to exchange value.
platform.simpleMeaning: A place where people connect online.

artificial intelligence.definition: Technology that helps computers perform tasks like thinking or assisting users.

worksheet.title: Understanding Chapter 1
worksheet.prompt:
1. What is a platform?

2. Name two ways digital platforms are part of daily life.

ch2.title: Chapter 2: What Is a Platform?
content: A [[platform]] is a place where people meet and exchange value.
A shopping platform connects sellers with buyers.
A learning platform connects teachers with learners.
Platforms act like bridges between groups.

platform.definition: A system that connects people and helps them interact.
platform.simpleMeaning: A system that brings people together.
platform.example: A marketplace app is a platform.

worksheet.title: What Makes a Platform?
worksheet.prompt: 1. In your own words, explain what a platform is.
